content:
Why Nub doesn’t want to replace Node. It wants to fix the experience around it.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

No member, click here
A few days ago, a new JavaScript toolchain quietly started making rounds in developer circles. It is called Nub.
At first glance it might look like just another “faster CLI tool”, but the idea behind it is slightly more interesting than that. Instead of trying to invent a new runtime or replace Node entirely, it leans in the opposite direction and tries to make Node itself feel more modern to work with.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Not another runtime experiment
The JavaScript ecosystem has spent the last few years splitting into two familiar directions.
One direction tries to replace Node completely. Bun is probably the clearest example here, bundling runtime, package manager, test runner, and tooling into a single unified system.
Another direction rebuilds everything from scratch with a different philosophy. Deno follows this path, focusing on security defaults, TypeScript support, and a redesigned standard library…
